verdict_summary: Conditional: registry-backed Traefik writer is the right Track 2 direction to replace docker-gen, but the current agent does not yet meet repo honesty bars for claiming ingress failover — fix die/exit0 misclassification, dual-writer ownership of failover-fallbacks.yaml, and do not claim success until whoami proof; treat always-on remote Docker replicas as a separate unproven dependency.

findings.title: die exit 0 treated as crash
findings.why_it_matters: An intentional compose stop emits die with exit code 0 before or instead of a clean stop classification; the agent marks crashed and peer-redeploys, violating the plan's crash≠intentional contract and creating unwanted replicas.
findings.suggested_fix: In onCrash (and classifyStopReason), inspect ExitCode/OOMKilled; if ExitCode==0 and not OOMKilled, route to intentional-stop path and skip EnsureReplicas.
evidence: On intentional stop ... does not peer-redeploy

findings.title: Dual writers of failover-fallbacks.yaml
findings.why_it_matters: osvc_ingress_sync.py and failover-agent both atomically write the same path; whichever runs last wins and can wipe crash-persisted registry routes or OpenSVC node lists mid-flight.
findings.suggested_fix: Declare failover-agent sole owner of failover-fallbacks.yaml in Compose comments and docs; add FAILOVER_DISABLE_OSVC_SYNC note; document that osvc_ingress_sync must not run concurrently on the same node.

findings.title: Peer replica ensure is unproven
findings.why_it_matters: Always-on HA replicas require Tailscale-reachable Docker API and recreate via ExportContainerConfig, which drops compose networks/secrets/bind semantics; peers will not faithfully match main without shared compose.
findings.suggested_fix: Gate EnsureReplicas behind explicit FAILOVER_REPLICA_ENSURE=true (default false until peer Docker + compose-up path proven); keep Traefik peer routes independent of replica ensure.
evidence: Remote Docker over Tailscale hostname:port — peers must expose...

findings.title: File routers omit middleware chains
findings.why_it_matters: When Docker-provider routes vanish after crash, file-provider Host(service.domain) routers without tinyauth/crowdsec chains can serve or forward without the policy stack STRATEGY requires for meaning preservation.
findings.suggested_fix: Copy traefik.http.routers.*.middlewares labels into file routers when present; otherwise set explicit priority below docker and document residual risk for unprotected services.
evidence: routing, auth, middleware, and health semantics should survive failover
traefik_writer emits routers with only rule/service/entryPoints

findings.title: Overclaims Implemented without whoami proof
findings.why_it_matters: STRATEGY forbids claiming failover success from generation alone; calling the brainstorm Implemented before kill-whoami proof recreates the false-confidence failure mode Track 2 warns about.
findings.suggested_fix: Change status to 'Code landed — runtime whoami proof pending; do not claim Track 2 closed'.
evidence: Status: Implemented (Compose-first failover-agent v1) — runtime whoami proof pending
claiming the repo already has universal multi-node failover

findings.title: HTTP-only filter missing vs osvc
findings.why_it_matters: Containers with only traefik.tcp.* and traefik.enable=true can enter HTTP failover YAML and create bogus HTTP routers for Redis/Mongo-style services.
findings.suggested_fix: Require at least one traefik.http.* label before registry upsert, matching osvc_ingress_sync.py.
evidence: Those must NOT be included here (osvc TCP filter comment)

residual_risks: Traefik healthCheck against https://svc.peer.domain may need serversTransport insecureSkipVerify / Host header behavior verification
Weight fields without explicit strategy may be ignored on older Traefik; osvc pattern uses ordered servers only
Auth continuity still depends on peer Traefik stack parity even after middleware copy
deferred_questions: Should replica ensure wait for a compose-up-on-peer path with shared repo checkout?
Is OpenSVC ingress sync retired on main host once failover-agent owns the file?
